description:
You are working as a performance engineer in charge of periodic housekeeping for raw
    profiling output that developers leave scattered throughout the shared project tree.

    1. Project layout that already exists  
       (all paths are absolute – do **not** create these, they are already on disk):  
       • /home/user/projects/benchmarks/app1/profiles/2023-01-15_run1.perf  
       • /home/user/projects/benchmarks/app2/perf_logs/performance.perf  
       • /home/user/projects/benchmarks/legacy/old_perf.perf  
       • /home/user/projects/benchmarks/newapp/newrun.perf       ← this file is **newer** than 30 days and must be left untouched.  
       • /home/user/projects/benchmarks/app1/readme.txt          ← not a “.perf” file, ignore.  

    2. Archival policy you must enforce  
       a. Any file with extension “.perf” whose modification time is **more than 30 days
          in the past** must be compressed *in-place* with gzip so that the resulting file
          has the same pathname plus “.gz” appended.  
          Example: “…/run1.perf  → …/run1.perf.gz”  
          After a successful compression the original “.perf” file must be completely gone
          (deleted).  
       b. Files that are 30 days old **or newer** must remain exactly as they are.  
       c. Non-“.perf” files are never touched.

    3. Summary log that MUST be produced  
       Create (or overwrite) the file  
       /home/user/archival_logs/compression_summary.log  

       • One CSV record per compressed file, followed by a final “TOTAL” line.  
       • Each per-file record has the following fields (no header line):  

         ABSOLUTE_PATH_TO_COMPRESSED_FILE,ORIGINAL_SIZE_BYTES,COMPRESSED_SIZE_BYTES

         – Absolute path: the *new* “.gz” file (not the deleted *.perf* file).  
         – Original size: size in **bytes** of the *.perf* file *before* compression.  
         – Compressed size: size in **bytes** of the resulting *.gz* file directly after
           gzip finishes.  
       • After the last file record, append a single line:

         TOTAL_COMPRESSED,<N>

         where <N> is the number of files you actually compressed.  
       • Every line (including the TOTAL line) ends with a single newline “\n”.  
       • No extra blank lines anywhere in the file.

       Example format if two files had been compressed (values shown only to illustrate
       the required shape – the numbers will obviously differ on your run):

         /home/user/projects/benchmarks/app1/profiles/2023-01-15_run1.perf.gz,240,108
         /home/user/projects/benchmarks/app2/perf_logs/performance.perf.gz,5120,1547
         TOTAL_COMPRESSED,2

       The automated grader will independently calculate file sizes and verify *both*
       sizes in every record as well as the correctness of the TOTAL line, so your log
       has to be **exactly** accurate.

    4. Technical constraints  
       • You do **not** have super-user (root) privileges.  
       • You must rely exclusively on standard GNU utilities that are already present in
         a typical Linux environment (e.g., find, xargs, gzip, stat, wc, etc.).  
       • The task is designed so that a single shell session is sufficient; there is no
         need for background daemons or cron jobs.  
       • Ensure that /home/user/archival_logs exists (create it if necessary) so that the
         summary file can be written there.

    5. Success criteria that the grader will check  
       a. Exactly three files must have been compressed:  
          • /home/user/projects/benchmarks/app1/profiles/2023-01-15_run1.perf.gz  
          • /home/user/projects/benchmarks/app2/perf_logs/performance.perf.gz  
          • /home/user/projects/benchmarks/legacy/old_perf.perf.gz  
       b. Their original *.perf* counterparts must no longer exist.  
       c. /home/user/projects/benchmarks/newapp/newrun.perf must still be present and
          uncompressed.  
       d. compression_summary.log exists, is readable by the current user, and conforms
          100 % to the format rules laid out in section 3.  
       e. The TOTAL_COMPRESSED number in the summary file must equal 3.  

    DO NOT paste literal command lines here; you will run commands interactively in the
    terminal to fulfil the above specification. The grader will open the summary log and
    inspect the filesystem to verify your work.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the automated grader.
        These values MUST NOT be revealed in the public task description.
    -->

    <!-- Files that already exist when the agent starts (with their full content). -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/projects/benchmarks/app1/profiles/2023-01-15_run1.perf">
PERF SAMPLE DATA FILE 1
PERF SAMPLE DATA FILE 1
PERF SAMPLE DATA FILE 1
PERF SAMPLE DATA FILE 1
PERF SAMPLE DATA FILE 1
PERF SAMPLE DATA FILE 1
PERF SAMPLE DATA FILE 1
PERF SAMPLE DATA FILE 1
PERF SAMPLE DATA FILE 1
PERF SAMPLE DATA FILE 1
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/projects/benchmarks/app2/perf_logs/performance.perf">
# callgraph-flat
# event: cycles:u
# captured on CI runner
0.01  sys_openat
0.02  sys_read
0.97  app_compute
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/projects/benchmarks/legacy/old_perf.perf">
Legacy profiling dump
time=12345, cycles=67890
branch-misses=1024
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/projects/benchmarks/newapp/newrun.perf">
Raw profile – keep this one uncompressed!
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/projects/benchmarks/app1/readme.txt">
This directory holds profiling output for app1.
        </file>
    </initial_files>

    <!-- Directory that must exist for the agent to be able to write the summary. -->
    <dir path="/home/user/archival_logs" />

    <!-- Files that must exist after the agent is done (original *.perf* deleted). -->
    <expected_final_state>
        <compressed_files>
            <file path="/home/user/projects/benchmarks/app1/profiles/2023-01-15_run1.perf.gz" original_size="240" />
            <file path="/home/user/projects/benchmarks/app2/perf_logs/performance.perf.gz" original_size="109" />
            <file path="/home/user/projects/benchmarks/legacy/old_perf.perf.gz" original_size="71" />
        </compressed_files>

        <!-- the untouched file -->
        <file path="/home/user/projects/benchmarks/newapp/newrun.perf" />

        <!-- summary log -->
        <file path="/home/user/archival_logs/compression_summary.log">
<!-- The grader will compute the exact content; it must match:
     <compressed_path_1>,240,<compressed_size_1>
     <compressed_path_2>,109,<compressed_size_2>
     <compressed_path_3>,71,<compressed_size_3>
     TOTAL_COMPRESSED,3
-->
        </file>
    </expected_final_state>
